stem: What is recurrence for worst case of QuickSort and what is the time complexity in Worst case?

options.label: A
options.text: Recurrence is T(n) = T(n − 2) + O(n) and time complexity is O(n²).

options.label: B
options.text: Recurrence is T(n) = T(n − 1) + O(n) and time complexity is O(n²).

options.label: C
options.text: Recurrence is T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n) and time complexity is O(n Log (n)).

options.label: D
options.text: Recurrence is T(n) = T(n/10) + T(9n/10) + O(n) and time complexity is O(n Log(n)).

explanation:
The worst case of QuickSort occurs when the picked pivot is always one of the corner elements in sorted array. In worst case, QuickSort recursively calls one subproblem with size 0 and other subproblem with size (n-1). So the recurrence is:

T(n) = T(n-1) + T(0) + O(n)

And the time complexity is: T(n) = ∑ᵢ₌₁ⁿ i = n(n+1)/2 = O(n²)

stem: What is time complexity of fun()?
explanation: For a input integer n, the innermost statement of fun() is executed following times. n + n/2 + n/4 + ... 1 So time complexity T(n) can be written as T(n) = O(n + n/2 + n/4 + ... 1) = O(n) The value of count is also n + n/2 + n/4 + .. + 1

stem: Which of the following sorting algorithms has the lowest worst-case complexity?
explanation:
Worst case complexities for the sorting algorithms are as follows:
Merge Sort — n Log(n).
Bubble Sort — n^2.
Quick Sort — n^2.
Selection Sort — n^2.

stem: Which of the following algorithm design technique is used in finding all pairs of shortest distances in a graph?
explanation:
Floyd Warshall Algorithm is the All Pairs Shortest Path problem which uses Dynamic Programming to find shortest distances between every pair of vertices in a given edge weighted directed Graph.
OR Bellman-Ford algorithm when there is only one source.

stem: Which of the following is false?

options.label: C
options.text: If 0 < x < y then nˣ = O(nʸ)

explanation: (See old notes for the basics)

stem: What is the height of a recurrence tree?

options.label: A
options.text: Equals 1 + the number of edges between the node and the root.

options.label: B
options.text: The number of edges on the longest path between a node and a leaf.

options.label: C
options.text: The number of edges on the longest path from the tree’s root and deepest leaf.

options.label: D
options.text: The number of edges from the tree's root node to the node.

explanation: A = level of a node. B = height of a node. C = height of the root. D = depth of a node.

stem: Which protocol uses the routing algorithm Dijkstra?
explanation:
In Routing Algorithms:
• Link-State protocols (OSPF, IS-IS) use Dijkstra.
• Distance-Vector protocols (RIP, IGRP, BGP) use Bellman-Ford.

stem: Graph traversal is different from a tree traversal, because

options.label: D
options.text: None is true as tree is a subset of graph.

explanation: As trees do not have loops, they are easier to traverse.

stem: What data structure is used for breadth first traversal of a graph?
explanation: Queue is used for breadth first traversal whereas stack is used for depth first traversal.

stem: Maximum number of nodes in a binary tree with height k, where root is height 0, is
explanation:
If the root node is at height 0, then a binary tree can have at max 2ᵏ⁺¹ − 1 nodes.
For example: a binary tree of height 1, can have maximum 2¹⁺¹ − 1 = 3 nodes.
     r      --------- 0
    / \
  L   R    --------- 1

stem: Queue data structure works on
explanation: In queue, data item inserted first, will be available first and data item inserted last will be available in the last. FIFO stands for First In First Out and is a correct answer.

stem: How many swaps are required to sort the given array using bubble sort - { 2, 5, 1, 3, 4}
explanation: There will be 3 swaps in first iteration and 1 swap in second iteration.

stem: In doubly linked lists

options.label: A
options.text: A pointer is maintained to store both next and previous nodes.

options.label: B
options.text: Two pointers are maintained to store next and previous nodes.

options.label: C
options.text: A pointer to self is maintained for each node.

explanation: One pointer variable can not store more than one address values.

stem: From a complete graph, by removing maximum _________ edges, we can construct a spanning tree.
explanation: We can remove maximum e−n+1 edges to get a spanning tree from complete graph. Any more deletion of edges will lead the graph to be disconnected.

stem: If the array is already sorted, which of these algorithms will exhibit the best performance
explanation: Insertion sort, as it should then work in linear way.

stem: Which of the following algorithm is not stable?
explanation: Among the given, only quick sort is not stable that is it may re-arrange the already sorted items.

stem: Which of the following has search efficiency of O(1)
explanation: A simple hash table has the Ω(1) efficiency.

stem: Which one of the below mentioned is linear data structure –
explanation: All mentioned data structures are linear in nature.

stem: Time complexity of Depth First Traversal of is
explanation: Using Depth First Search, we traverse the whole graph i.e. visiting all Vertices and Edges.

stem: In context with time-complexity, find the odd out –
explanation: Binary heap heapify operation has time-complexity of O(n), while other operations have time-complexity of O(1) only.

stem: Shell sort uses
explanation: Shell sort uses insertion sort when interval value is 1.

stem: All possible spanning trees of graph G

options.label: A
options.text: Have same number of edges and vertices.

options.label: B
options.text: Have same number of edges and but not vertices.

options.label: C
options.text: Have same number of vertices but not edges.

explanation: Review Spanning Trees.

stem: Which of the following algorithm cannot be designed without recursion –
explanation: Every problem which can be solved using recursion can also be solved using iterations.

stem: What is the worst-case run-time complexity of binary search algorithm?
explanation: In the worst case, binary search will be left or right intended, making it compare all the n values.

stem: Which of the following uses memorization?
explanation: Remembering the results of previously calculated solutions is called memoization.

stem: What is the worst case run-time complexity of binary search algorithm?
explanation: In the worst case, binary search will be left or right intended, making it compare all the n values.

stem: Heap is an example of:
explanation: A heap is a complete binary tree with the following ordering property: The value of every parent node is greater than or equal to the values of either of its daughter nodes.

stem: What do we use Dijkstra algorithm for?

options.label: A
options.text: Find the number of vertices there are in a given graph.

options.label: B
options.text: Find the shortest path between two vertices on a weighted graph.

options.label: C
options.text: Find the number of edges there are in a given graph.

options.label: D
options.text: Find the path between a single node, on a weighted graph, to all other nodes in the graph.

explanation: Read about Dijkstra.

stem: What type of circuit is illustrated in to Fig 4.5.1?
explanation: Data Selector is the same as a Multiplexer

stem: The time required for a gate or inverter to change its state is called:
explanation:
Rise time: for a pulse to change from 10 to 90 percent of its maximum value.
Decay time: for a pulse to decrease from 90 to 10 per cent of its maximum value.

stem: The number of full and half-adders required to add 16-bit numbers is:
explanation: The one half-adder can add the least significant bit of the two numbers. Full adders are required to add the remaining 15 bits as they all involve adding carries.

stem: A demultiplexer is used to:

options.label: A
options.text: Route the data from single input to one of many outputs

options.label: D
options.text: Select data from several inputs and route it to single output

explanation:
In demultiplexer, inputs is inserted serially and then it gives multiple outputs which are in parallel form.
Answer (D) is a multiplexer.

stem: In ______ each station sends a frame whenever it has a frame to send.
explanation:
Pure ALOHA and Slotted ALOHA both are the Random Access Protocols, that are implemented on the Medium Access Control (MAC) layer, a sublayer of Data Link Layer. The purpose of the ALOHA protocol is to determine that which competing station must get the next chance of accessing the multi-access channel at MAC layer. The main difference between Pure ALOHA and Slotted ALOHA is that the time in Pure Aloha is continuous whereas, the time in Slotted ALOHA is discrete.

Comparison chart: https://techdifferences.com/difference-between-pure-aloha-and-slotted-aloha.html

stem: The Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) is a communications protocol used for:

options.label: A
options.text: Assisting the users to configure multiple network devices from a single source

options.label: B
options.text: Mapping of Internet layer addresses into link layer addresse

options.label: C
options.text: Assisting the users by helping to link between common names to an IP address and back.

options.label: D
options.text: Transmitting files between computers on the internet over TCP/IP connections

explanation: A is DHCP --- C is DNS --- D is FTP --- More info: https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/Network_Plus_Certification/Technologies/Common_Protocols

stem: Which network protocol uses out-of-band control?
explanation: Out-of-band control is a characteristic of network protocols with which data control is regulated. Out-of-band control passes control data on a separate connection from main data.

stem: When both tables can have only one record on either side of the relationship, this is known as:
explanation: See all types of relationships in Database.

explanation: Review and Study UML Diagrams.

explanation: Review and Study UML Diagrams.

stem: In UML diagrams, relationship between object and component parts is represented by
explanation: Review and Study UML Diagrams.

stem: What is a hash table?

options.label: A
options.text: A structure that maps values to keys.

options.label: B
options.text: A structure that maps keys to values.

options.label: D
options.text: A structure used to implement stack and queue.

explanation: A hash table is used to implement an associative array, which has a key-value pair. So, the hash table maps keys to values.

stem: If several elements are competing for the same bucket in the hash table, what is it called?
explanation: Review and study Hash tables.

stem: In a hash table, what is direct addressing?

options.label: A
options.text: Distinct array position for every possible key.

explanation: Direct addressing is possible only when we can afford to allocate an array that has one position for every possible key.

stem: What is the search complexity in direct addressing?
explanation: Since every key has a unique array position, searching takes a constant time.

stem: What is a hash function?

options.label: A
options.text: A function has allocated memory to keys.

options.label: B
options.text: A function that computes the location of the key in the array.

options.label: C
options.text: A function that creates an array.

explanation: In a hash table, there are fewer array positions than the keys, so the position of the key in the array has to be computed, this is done using the hash function.

stem: What can be the techniques to avoid collision?

options.label: A
options.text: Make the hash function appear random.

explanation: Making the hash function random is not really a good choice, although it is considered one of the techniques to avoid collisions along with chaining and simple uniform hashing.

stem: What is the load factor?
explanation: In simple chaining, load factor is the average number of elements stored in a chain, and is given by the ratio of number of elements stored to the number of slots in the array.

stem: What is simple uniform hashing?

options.label: A
options.text: Every element has equal probability of hashing into any of the slots.

options.label: B
options.text: A weighted probabilistic method is used to hash elements into the slots.

explanation: In simple uniform hashing, any given element is equally likely to hash into any of the slots available in the array.

stem: In simple uniform hashing, what is the search complexity?
explanation: There are two cases, once when the search is successful and when it is unsuccessful, but in both the cases, the complexity is O(1+alpha) where 1 is to compute the hash function and alpha is the load factor.

stem: In simple chaining, what data structure is appropriate?
explanation: Deletion becomes easier with doubly linked list, hence it is appropriate.

stem: Which of the following gives a logical structure of the database graphically?
explanation: E-R diagrams are simple and clear—qualities that may well account in large part for the widespread use of the E-R model.

stem: The entity relationship set is represented in E-R diagram as:
explanation: Dashed lines link attributes of a relationship set to the relationship set.

stem: The Rectangles divided into two parts represents:
explanation: The first part of the rectangle , contains the name of the entity set. The second part contains the names of all the attributes of the entity set.

stem: Consider a directed line (→) from the relationship set advisor to both entity sets instructor and student. This indicates __________ cardinality.
explanation: This indicates that an instructor may advise at most one student, and a student may have at most one advisor.

stem: We indicate roles in E-R diagrams by labeling the lines that connect ____________ to ___________.
explanation: Diamond represents a relationship set and rectangle represents a entity set.

stem: An entity set that does not have sufficient attributes to form a primary key is termed a ___________.
explanation: An entity set that has a primary key is termed a strong entity set.

stem: For a weak entity set to be meaningful, it must be associated with another entity set, called the:
explanation: Every weak entity must be associated with an identifying entity; that is, the weak entity set is said to be existence dependent on the identifying entity set. The identifying entity set is said to own the weak entity set that it identifies.It is also called as owner entity set.

stem: Weak entity set is represented as:
explanation: An entity set that has a primary key is termed a strong entity set.

stem: If you were collecting and storing information about your music collection, an album would be considered a(n) ______.
explanation: An entity set is a logical container for instances of an entity type and instances of any type derived from that entity type.

stem: What term is used to refer to a specific record in your music database; for instance; information stored about a specific album?
explanation: The environment of database is said to be instance. A database instance or an ‘instance’ is made up of the background processes needed by the database.

stem: Average memory access time can be calculated as:

options.label: A
options.text: Average memory access time = Hit time + (Miss rate * Miss penalty).

options.label: B
options.text: Average memory access time = Hit time – (Miss rate * Miss penalty).

options.label: C
options.text: Average memory access time = Hit time + (Miss rate / Miss penalty).

options.label: D
options.text: Average memory access time = Miss rate / Miss penalty.

explanation: Review and Study AMAT.

stem: What is the throughput of simple/pure ALOHA?
explanation: Pure ALOHA is 18.4%. Slotted ALOHA is 36.8%. Please review and Study AMAT.
